description:
This rule configures the system to lock out accounts during a specified time period after a
number of incorrect login attempts using <tt>pam_tally2.so</tt>.
rationale:
By limiting the number of failed logon attempts, the risk of unauthorized system access via
user password guessing, also known as brute-forcing, is reduced. Limits are imposed by locking
the account.
ocil_clause: unlock_time is less than the expected value
ocil:
To ensure the failed password attempt policy is configured correctly, run the following command: 
$ grep pam_tally2 /etc/pam.d/login
The output should show unlock_time=<interval-in-seconds> where interval-in-seconds is or greater.
fixtext:
To configure the system to lock out accounts during a specified time period after a number of incorrect login attempts using pam_faillock.so, Modify the content of both /etc/pam.d/login, like this: 
auth required pam_tally2.so deny= unlock_time=
srg_requirement: Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9 must automatically lock an account until the locked account is released by an administrator.
title: Set Lockout Time for Failed Password Attempts using pam_tally2
